summary: Vintage soundboard recording (with brief dropouts) captures a stellar 1972 set highlighted by a blazing Truckin' → Dark Star → Comes A Time sequence and standout renditions of Jack Straw, Stella Blue and Play In The Band.
review: The show is praised as pure 1972 magic with pristine vocals, tight playing and memorable jams – especially the back‑to‑back Garcia tunes and the monster Truckin'/Dark Star/Comes A Time stretch. The soundboard source sounds clear overall, though it drops out toward the end of the performance.

ai_rating.rationale: Enthusiastic 5‑star review highlights exceptional musical performance and mostly good soundboard quality, with only minor dropout issues affecting the rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear vintage soundboard recording; noticeable dropouts toward the end of the set.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set described as two late‑80s‑style sets; back‑to‑back Garcia numbers and a seamless Truckin' → Dark Star → Comes A Time jam give the concert strong momentum.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Lost some lyrics during 'Loser' but delivered a beautiful Dark Star, taking the jam extra mile.
band_member_comments.Phil: Shows strong vocal presence on Brown Eyed Women, singing with gusto.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith's lighter boogie‑woogie touch shines, especially on 'B.I.O.D.T.L.'.
